inclusion_criteria: Study designs: observational (cross-sectional, cohort, case-control) or interventional trials that measure child growth and report stunting as an outcome.
Population: children aged 0–59 months residing in low- and middle-income countries (per World Bank classification at the time of the study).
Comparator: includes a non-stunted group (height-for-age Z-score [HAZ] ≥ -2) or reports data enabling comparison with non-stunted children.
Outcomes: reports stunting status defined using WHO Child Growth Standards (HAZ < -2) or provides HAZ data from which this can be derived.
exclusion_criteria: Not conducted in LMICs, not focused on children under 5 years, or results cannot be disaggregated for this population/context.
Does not report stunting using WHO HAZ criteria and does not provide sufficient data to derive stunting or a non-stunted comparator.
Non-primary research (e.g., reviews, meta-analyses, editorials, commentaries, protocols) or conference abstracts without extractable primary data; non-human studies.
Focuses exclusively on other nutritional indicators (e.g., wasting, underweight) without reporting stunting outcomes.
screening_criteria.description: Screening criteria for studies assessing stunting (WHO HAZ < -2) among children under 5 in LMICs, requiring inclusion of or comparability to a non-stunted group (HAZ ≥ -2), across observational or interventional study designs.

Differences in growth trajectories in breastfed HIV-exposed uninfected and HIV-unexposed infants in Kenya: An observational cohort study.
Quantifying and exploiting the age dependence in the effect of supplementary food for child undernutrition.
Care groups in an integrated nutrition education intervention improved infant growth among South Sudanese refugees in Uganda's West Nile post-emergency settlements: A cluster randomized trial.
Effect of heat stress in the first 1000 days of life on fetal and infant growth: a secondary analysis of the ENID randomised controlled trial.
Prenatal and postnatal environmental influences on child growth trajectories across a rural-urban gradient: an analysis of the ECoMiD longitudinal birth cohort.
Household Food Insecurity, Growth and Development of Preschool Children: Evidence From Rural Pakistan.
Longitudinal Analysis of the Intestinal Microbiota in Persistently Stunted Young Children in South India.
Impact of facility based versus home based growth monitoring and promotion programs on child nutritional status in rural Bangladesh.
Longitudinal anthropometric assessment of infants born to HIV-1-infected mothers, Belo Horizonte, Southeastern Brazil.
Associations Between Micronutrient Status, Hormones, and Immune Status During Pregnancy and Child Growth in Rural Bangladesh: A Prospective Cohort Study.

Independent and combined effects of improved water, sanitation, and hygiene, and improved complementary feeding, on child stunting and anaemia in rural Zimbabwe: a cluster-randomised trial.

Correlation between gut microbiota composition, enteric infections and linear growth impairment: a case-control study in childhood stunting in Pidie, Aceh, Indonesia.
